compliance_gate.robots_txt: User-agent: * Disallow: / (only Facebook/Twitter preview bots allowed); AI training / RAG bot access explicitly prohibited
compliance_gate.verdict: Live scraping violates robots.txt and likely Naver/publisher terms. Fixtures below are for OFFLINE parser tests only. Production collection must go through the Naver Open API or an explicit legal/compliance approval.
compliance_gate.attribution_required: Publisher name + original article URL must be preserved in every record. Article body text must never be stored or republished from these pages.
compliance_gate.credentials: None used, none required, none permitted (no Naver API HUB secrets in this path).

encoding_notes.ranking_page: EUC-KR (decode as euc-kr; utf-8 decoding produces mojibake)

cursor_protocol.cursor_format: YYYYMMDDHHMMSS (KST timestamp, walks backward in time)

entertainment_ranking.rendering: SPA shell (~2KB), JavaScript-only, no embedded state payload
entertainment_ranking.action: defer; do not implement via HTML scraping

section_page.item_rank_in_headline_block: a.sa_text_title[data-nlog-params] JSON key 'rank' (strip before fixture storage; record derived integer only)

ranking_page_fixture.description: Sanitized snapshot of popularDay.naver for offline parser tests

section_page_fixture.description: Sanitized snapshot of /section/{sid} (or a SECTION_ARTICLE_LIST fragment) for offline parser tests

position.note: document order; equals data-nlog rank within headline block when present

sanitization_rules: Strip all tracking/analytics attributes before storing fixture HTML or derived records: nclicks(...), data-nlog-area, data-nlog-params, data-imp-gdid, data-imp-index, data-clk, data-ticket.

Store derived structured records (the field schemas above), not raw HTML, wherever possible; if raw HTML snippets are kept for parser tests, truncate to the minimal block and apply the attribute stripping rule.
Never store article body text, comments, comment counts attributable to users, or any user-identifying data.
Cap fixture size: max 3 publisher groups (ranking) / max 10 articles (section) per fixture file.
Always record fetched_at, source_url, and encoding so staleness is auditable.
Preserve publisher attribution (publisher name + original URL) in every record.
Fixtures live under tests fixtures only; they must not feed production content generation.
known_risks: Selectors are unversioned Naver internals and can change without notice; parser must fail soft into the project's fallback chain (cache -> settings keywords -> placeholder).
Ranking page is per-publisher top-5 with no category filter; category coverage requires section pages instead.
Section timestamps are relative Korean strings; absolute times are not available on list pages.
Entertainment category is not obtainable from these routes (SPA-only).
robots.txt prohibits generic and AI-agent crawling; this contract does not authorize live collection.
